Hub Home > design-and-branding > Logo Concept Brief Generator

Logo Concept Brief Generator

Generate a detailed creative brief for a logo design, including style direction, color psychology rationale, typography recommendations, and 5 distinct concept descriptions ready to hand to a designer or feed into an AI image tool.

Beginner PASTE & GO Brand-building
Pro tip

Do NOT paste this into Midjourney or DALL-E directly. Use the AI to write the brief first, then translate each concept description into tool-specific image prompts. A brief-first approach produces far more cohesive results than prompt-and-pray.

logo branding design brief identity visual identity

Prompt Variants by Model

Claude Claude 4.x
FRESH APR 2026 
You are a brand identity designer creating a logo concept brief for a small business.

<business_context>
Business name: [NAME]
Industry: [INDUSTRY]
What we do: [ONE SENTENCE DESCRIPTION]
Target...
You are a brand identity designer creating a logo concept brief for a small business.

<business_context>
Business name: [NAME]
Industry: [INDUSTRY]
What we do: [ONE SENTENCE DESCRIPTION]
Target...

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Your email unlocks all 74 prompts instantly. No password, no signup — just your email. 

You are a brand identity designer creating a logo concept brief for a small business.

<business_context>
Business name: [NAME]
Industry: [INDUSTRY]
What we do: [ONE SENTENCE DESCRIPTION]
Target audience: [WHO BUYS FROM YOU]
Competitors: [2-3 COMPETITOR NAMES]
Personality traits: [e.g., "trustworthy, modern, approachable" — pick 3]
Must avoid: [ANY VISUAL DIRECTIONS TO AVOID]
Where the logo will appear: [WEBSITE / BUSINESS CARDS / SIGNAGE / APP ICON / ALL]
</business_context>

Generate a logo concept brief with:

1. **Style direction:** Recommend one primary style (wordmark, lettermark, symbol, combination mark, emblem) with reasoning based on the business type and where the logo appears most.

2. **Color palette:** Recommend a primary color and two supporting colors. For each, explain the psychological association and why it fits this business. Provide exact hex codes. Include a dark and light background version.

3. **Typography direction:** Recommend 2-3 font categories (not specific fonts — categories like "geometric sans-serif" or "humanist serif") with reasoning. Specify whether the logotype should feel heavy/bold, medium, or light.

4. **5 distinct concepts:** For each concept provide:
   - Concept name (2-3 words)
   - Visual description (what someone would see — be specific about shapes, arrangement, negative space)
   - Mood/feeling it evokes
   - One-sentence rationale connecting it to the business
   - A detailed image prompt (80-120 words) written for AI image generation tools

5. **Usage guidance:** Minimum size recommendations, spacing rules, what NOT to do with the logo (common mistakes).

Be specific and visual in your descriptions. A designer should be able to sketch from your concept descriptions without asking follow-up questions.
Notes: Claude excels at structured creative briefs. The XML context block ensures nothing is missed. Use Projects to store the brief and iterate across sessions.
ChatGPT GPT-5
FRESH APR 2026 
Act as a brand identity designer with 15 years of experience working with startups and small businesses. Create a comprehensive logo concept brief.

**The Business:**

Your email unlocks all 74 prompts instantly. No password, no signup — just your email. 

Act as a brand identity designer with 15 years of experience working with startups and small businesses. Create a comprehensive logo concept brief.

**The Business:**
- Name: [NAME]
- Industry: [INDUSTRY]
- What we do: [ONE SENTENCE]
- Target audience: [WHO BUYS]
- Competitors: [2-3 NAMES]
- Brand personality: [3 TRAITS]
- Avoid: [VISUAL DIRECTIONS TO AVOID]
- Logo appears on: [WHERE]

**Deliver:**

1. **Style recommendation:** Wordmark, lettermark, symbol, combination, or emblem — and why.
2. **Color palette:** Primary + 2 supporting colors with hex codes, psychological reasoning, and light/dark background versions.
3. **Typography:** 2-3 font category recommendations (not specific fonts) with reasoning.
4. **5 concepts:** Each with a name, detailed visual description, mood, rationale, and an 80-120 word image generation prompt.
5. **Usage rules:** Minimum sizes, spacing, common mistakes to avoid.

Make every description specific enough that a designer could sketch it without questions.
Notes: Use DALL-E integration to generate rough concept visualizations directly from the image prompts. Iterate on the strongest concept.

I need a professional logo concept brief for my small business.

**About the business:**
- Name: [NAME]
- Industry: [INDUSTRY]
- What we do: [DESCRIPTION]
- Who we serve: [TARGET AUDIENCE]
- Competitors: [2-3 NAMES]
- Brand personality: [3 TRAITS]
- Avoid: [WHAT NOT TO DO]
- Logo used on: [WHERE]

**Create a brief with:**
1. Recommended logo style (wordmark/lettermark/symbol/combination/emblem) with reasoning
2. Color palette — primary + 2 accent colors with hex codes and psychological reasoning
3. Typography direction — font categories with rationale
4. 5 distinct logo concepts, each with: concept name, detailed visual description, mood, business rationale, and an 80-120 word prompt for AI image generation
5. Usage guidelines — minimum sizes, spacing, common mistakes

Be visually specific in every description.
Notes: Gemini can research competitor logos in real-time to inform differentiation. Ask it to look up competitor visual identities before generating concepts.

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Your email unlocks all 74 prompts instantly. No password, no signup — just your email. 

Help me create a logo concept brief for my business.

**My business:**
- Name: [NAME]
- Industry: [INDUSTRY]
- What we do: [DESCRIPTION]
- Our customers: [TARGET AUDIENCE]
- Competitors: [2-3 NAMES]
- Our brand personality: [3 WORDS]
- What to avoid: [DIRECTIONS WE DO NOT WANT]
- Where the logo goes: [WEBSITE / CARDS / SIGNAGE / ETC]

**I need:**
1. What type of logo I should go with (wordmark, symbol, combination, etc.) and why
2. A color palette — one main color, two supporting colors, with hex codes and why each color works for my business
3. What kind of fonts to look for (general style, not specific fonts)
4. 5 different logo ideas — for each one, give me a name, describe exactly what it looks like, what feeling it gives off, why it works for my business, and write a detailed prompt I can use in an AI image tool
5. Basic rules for using the logo — minimum sizes, spacing, common mistakes

Make the descriptions specific enough that a designer could draw them without asking me questions.
Notes: Copilot with Designer can generate logo concepts directly. Use the image prompts from this brief as starting points.
